# Contributing to Redolarium

First off, thank you for considering contributing to Redolarium! It's people like you that make Redolarium such a great tool for genomic analysis.

## Where do I go from here?

If you've noticed a bug or have a feature request, make sure to check our [Issues](https://github.com/mini-mator/Redolarium/issues) to see if someone else has already created an issue for it. If not, go ahead and make one!

## Fork & create a branch

If this is something you think you can fix, then fork Redolarium and create a branch with a descriptive name.

## Get the test suite running

Make sure your changes don't break the existing pipeline by running the Redolarium tests.

## Implement your fix or feature

At this point, you're ready to make your changes! Feel free to ask for help; everyone is a beginner at first.

## Make a Pull Request

At this point, you should switch back to your master branch and make sure it's up to date with Redolarium's master branch.
Then, create a Pull Request with a clear description of the problem and solution. Include the relevant issue number if applicable.

## Biological Accuracy (Critical)
Redolarium is a peer-review-grade pipeline. Any changes to biological heuristics, statistical thresholds (e-values, identity %, z-scores), or mechanistic interpretations MUST be backed by a peer-reviewed citation (DOI preferred). See the main `AGENTS.md` rules for more details.
